notes:
Opening-phase private work notes for plaintiff, arb-ex11a-20260630135427.

Objective: prove by preponderance that by May 24, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, Iran initiated a major non-weather closure of its airspace within the market rule.

Opportunity: openings:plaintiff. Final action allowed: record_opening_statement only. No evidence submission or technical reports permitted in this phase.

Record inspection:
- get_case/list_evidence show one visible case-packet evidence item: ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, title market-page.txt, text/plain, 4971 bytes, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17, system case_packet.
- stat_evidence confirmed metadata and read limits.
- read_evidence_range read entire item. Market rule requires a Yes if Iran initiated a major non-weather closure of its airspace by listed date 11:59 PM ET. Major closure includes broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region; limited exceptions do not disqualify. It also includes non-weather complete suspension of commercial arrivals/departures affecting at least two listed airports: IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN. Warnings or restrictions imposed by airlines/other countries do not suffice. Primary resolution sources are official Iranian aviation info and consensus of credible reporting.
- The packet itself proves the rule and market metadata, not the occurrence of the May 24 closure.

Source map:
- Primary target: Iranian Civil Aviation Organization / Iran Airports and Air Navigation Company NOTAM text, reported by later search leads as OIIX A1010/26, issued May 22 and valid to May 25, concerning western Tehran FIR/OIIX and airport restrictions.
- Confirming targets: flight-tracking reroute data, SafeAirspace/OPSGROUP/EASA operational summaries, Reuters/AP/RFE/RL/Indian Express or other credible reporting identifying the NOTAM and operational effects.
- Adverse targets: material showing only airline/country warnings, weather restrictions, VFR-only or narrow local closures, or continued commercial ops too broad to treat the restriction as a major closure.

Native investigation completed before opening:
- Web searches: Iran airspace closure May 24 2026 commercial flights Iran Civil Aviation Organization; site:cao.ir Iran airspace May 24 2026 closure flights; Iran closes airspace May 24 2026 Reuters FlightRadar24; Iran NOTAM airspace closed May 24 2026; exact phrase searches for May 22/May 25/western airspace/NOTAM; Iran eight airports daylight May 24 airspace.
- Opened SafeAirspace Iran page: useful background/adverse context about OIIX closures/reopenings and warnings, but not direct May 24 proof.
- Opened EASA CZIB 2026-03-R13: official aviation safety context; external warning, not Iranian closure itself.
- Opened OPSGROUP Middle East operational picture: operational background; not direct proof of May 22 NOTAM.
- Opened Indian Express May 23 report: secondary report states Iran temporarily closed western half from May 22 to May 25, suspended civil flight permits in western Tehran FIR, identifies NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, only eight airports operational, daylight-only, per-flight CAA approval, flight-tracking reroutes. This is a prime candidate for later evidence if official NOTAM cannot be captured.
- Opened Washington Examiner May 22 report: states western airspace closed until Monday and CAA.Iran issued NOTAM; less detailed.
- Opened RFE/RL May 23 live item: states Iran Civil Aviation Organization announced temporary western airspace restrictions, only eight airports permitted, daylight-only, individual CAA permits.

Analysis:
- Plaintiff theory: a state-issued NOTAM closing the western Tehran FIR/OIIX through May 25, suspending civil flight permits there, and reducing operations elsewhere to eight daylight/per-permit airports is a major Iranian airspace-region closure. It is non-weather and Iran-initiated. If official NOTAM confirms that scope, it likely satisfies the rule even with limited exceptions.
- Defense theory: this was western-only and allowed continued operations at eight airports, so it was limited/partial. The plaintiff must tie the restriction to the rule's words: major Iranian airspace region, not necessarily entire national airspace, and limited exceptions do not disqualify.
- Weight/provenance: current record source is a system case packet and enough to establish rule language. Occurrence proof remains missing from admitted record. External reporting found in investigation cannot be cited as admitted proof until submitted in a later phase.

Durable notes saved to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with source URLs/identifiers and unresolved gaps.

Opening defendant work notes: Read opportunity prompt and current record. Checked get_case, list_evidence, stat_evidence, and read_evidence_range for ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17. Only admitted evidence is market-page.txt, a system case packet. It establishes rule language, examples, source hierarchy, and operative elements: Iran-initiated; non-weather; by 2026-05-24 23:59 ET; major closure defined as broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region, with limited partial closures not qualifying and limited exceptions to a broad closure not disqualifying; alternative complete suspension affecting at least two listed airports (IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN). It does not establish the May 22-25 factual event. Plaintiff opening concedes that gap but previews an official Iranian notice/NOTAM governing western Tehran FIR/OIIX.

Native investigation source map and search log: decisive elements are issuer, non-weather reason, effective time through deadline, exact scope, commercial flight effect, exceptions, and whether closure was broad/major. Searched web for May 24 2026 Iran airspace closure NOTAM Tehran FIR western sector; Iran closes airspace May 24 2026 western Tehran FIR OIIX; site:notams.aim.faa.gov Iran OIIX May 24 2026 airspace closure; Iran Civil Aviation Organization May 24 2026 airspace closed; exact searches for "OIIX A1010/26", "A1010/26" "OIIX", "western part" "Tehran FIR" "A1010/26", "all airports" "western part of the Tehran FIR". Leads found but not admitted: Notamify mirror of raw NOTAM A1010/26 says all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except listed airports with sunrise-to-sunset operations and new CAA permission for civil IFR passenger flights; Indian Express reports CAA NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, May 22-May 25, western half, only eight airports operational, daylight/per-flight approval, attributed to Gulf News; SafeAirspace/OPSGROUP/EASA provide context and foreign-advisory risk, not direct Iran-initiated closure proof. Need later evidence admission: official NOTAM source if possible, Notamify raw capture if primary unavailable, Gulf News/official statement, mapping ICAO codes to listed airports, time conversion, adverse reopening/no-new-NOTAM checks.

Reasoning and opening theory: Defense should not pretend no adverse leads exist; instead emphasize that current admitted record proves only the rule. The decisive issue is exact authenticated source text and scope. A NOTAM about airport operations in part of a FIR may or may not equal a major closure of airspace/commercial transit/arrivals/departures; exceptions, daylight operations, PPR/new-permission operations, and whether two listed airports had complete suspensions matter. Plaintiff carries burden by preponderance. Defense will concede that authenticated Iran-issued broad closure or complete suspension at two listed airports through the deadline would defeat us; absent that, snippets, foreign advisories, airline avoidance, and secondary chains are not enough. Cross-turn notes saved at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Merits argument private notes for plaintiff, arb-ex11a-20260630135427.

Opportunity: arguments:plaintiff. Final action: submit_argument. Evidence admission permitted.

Record refresh:
- get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence run at start. Before plaintiff submissions, record still contained only ev_e61df84283cc_market-page plus openings. Defense opening argued source/custody/scope. No new case-packet files or defense evidence.

Evidence submitted and accepted:
1. ev_ac2309bdc9f7_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-ac2309bdc9f7: Indian Express report, May 23 2026, captured text from https://indianexpress.com/article/world/us-news/iran-closes-airspace-trump-cancels-plans-us-military-strikes-rubio-india-10704692/. It reports Iran temporarily closed airspace over western half from May 22 to May 25, suspended all civil flight permits in western Tehran FIR/OIIX, identifies Iranian Civil Aviation Authority NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, says all airports in western Tehran FIR shut, and states only eight airports operational under daylight-only/per-flight approval restrictions.
2. ev_57917ec86ba3_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-57917ec86ba3: RFE/RL live report item timestamped 10:03 23.5.2026 from https://www.rferl.org/a/iran-war-us-hormuz-oil-blockade-gulf-israel/33640284/lbl0lbi456071.html. It reports Iran Civil Aviation Organization announced temporary restrictions over western airspace; latest NOTAM severe restrictions; eight airports permitted, daylight-only, individual CAA permits.
3. ev_b762826a22f0_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-b762826a22f0: SafeAirspace Iran page, https://safeairspace.net/iran/. Shows OIIX/Tehran FIR status and later context: eastern partial reopening, western remaining closed, security-risk warnings. Useful corroboration and adverse scope context, not direct May 22 proof.
4. ev_51569ad0cb54_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-51569ad0cb54: EASA CZIB 2026-03-R13, https://www.easa.europa.eu/en/domains/air-operations/czibs/2026-03-r13. Official safety bulletin showing non-weather security/conflict risk, Iran air-defense alert, state airspace closures/restrictions during conflict, recommendation not to operate in Iran/Iraq/Lebanon affected airspace.

Analysis:
- Market packet proves rule: major closure includes broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region; limited exceptions do not disqualify.
- The two contemporaneous reports align on issuer (Iran CAA/Civil Aviation Organization), NOTAM, effective dates (May 22-May 25), geographic scope (western Tehran FIR/OIIX), and operational limits (only eight airports, daylight-only, fresh permit per flight).
- Effective through May 25 necessarily covers May 24 11:59 PM ET under ordinary reading; exact timezone is not specified in reports, but validity to May 25 is comfortably beyond deadline unless contradicted by time-zone nuance.
- Non-weather nature is supported by reports describing military/security tensions and by EASA/SafeAirspace conflict-risk context.
- Provenance limitation: raw official NOTAM not captured. Need acknowledge weight issue; argue preponderance satisfied because independent reporting plus official aviation context converge, no adverse source found saying weather, foreign-only restrictions, or expired-before-deadline.
- Defense partiality point: rule allows major Iranian airspace region and limited exceptions; western Tehran FIR is major, and per-flight/daylight exceptions do not defeat broad closure.

Durable notes updated in /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant arguments work notes: Opportunity arguments:defendant ready. Checked current record, list_evidence, stat_evidence, and read_evidence_range for plaintiff exhibits. Plaintiff submitted four source captures: ev_ac2309bdc9f7 Indian Express (secondary report citing Gulf News, says CAA issued OIIX A1010/26 and describes western half airspace closure/all airports in western FIR closed; says eight airports operational incl Mehrabad/IKA/Isfahan/Shiraz/Yazd/Tabriz/Mashhad/Bandar Abbas); ev_57917ec86ba3 RFE/RL live report (says Iran Civil Aviation Organization announced temporary restrictions on western airspace, severe restrictions, eight airports permitted, daylight and individual permits; no NOTAM identifier); ev_b762826a22f0 SafeAirspace (June context: partial reopening eastern OIIX while western remains closed; March closure after strikes; risk context); ev_51569ad0cb54 EASA CZIB (official foreign safety bulletin; conflict/security risk; recommendation not operate in affected airspace; states many concerned states used temporary closures/restrictions). Plaintiff’s exhibits are helpful to Yes on context but still no official Iranian AIS/CAA source or raw NOTAM in plaintiff record.

Native investigation this phase: searched exact strings for A1010/26 and raw NOTAM wording plus no-new-NOTAM terms. Found Notamify pages for A1010/26 and replacement A1015/26. Submitted defendant evidence ev_241b442bed15_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-241b442bed15, sha256 241b442bed1566db9088fa4e9dd159b18a0afcafef11dc3cc63d5db9b4564b02. Source URL: https://notamify.com/notams/OIIX/693f2d0f-54d3-433b-8128-49f5475ba269 and https://notamify.com/notams/OIIX/67435351-52f9-4d17-8f4c-497a21dd94bf. Limit: unofficial mirror, not original Iranian AIS/CAA. Use it because plaintiff relies on the same NOTAM identifier and exact text matters.

D exhibit content: A1010/26 page category AD Airport Operations; period UTC 2026-05-22 20:46 to 2026-05-25 08:30; raw Q)OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/; text says ref A0796/26, all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, OIAW operational sunrise to sunset; all previous permissions suspended; new permission required from CAA for operation of civil IFR passenger flights on these airports. A1015/26 replaced A1010 on 2026-05-23 18:21 UTC through 2026-05-25 08:30; same QFALC/AD category; text says all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, OIAW, OIFM, OITL, OISR, OIAM operational sunrise to sunset; previous permissions suspended; new permission for civil IFR passenger flights from Iran CAA.

Merits theory: Argue plaintiff has shown restrictions, not necessarily rule-defined major closure. Market requires broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from airspace or major airspace region, or complete suspension of arrivals/departures at two listed airports. Raw mirrored NOTAM wording is aerodrome/airport operations (QFALC) and closes airports in west part of FIR with exceptions; it does not say FIR/airspace or overflights are closed. Plaintiff’s reports conflict with raw mirror on which airports were excepted/operational, undermining complete-suspension path for listed airports. PX-4/PX-5 are context/foreign advisories, not Iran-initiated proof. Need be candid: D evidence is unofficial and adverse in showing Iran did impose significant airport restrictions through deadline, but exact wording and source mismatch create enough doubt under plaintiff burden. Cross-turn notes updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff rebuttal private notes, arb-ex11a-20260630135427.

Opportunity: rebuttals:plaintiff. Final actions: submit_rebuttal or pass.

Record/evidence scan:
- list_evidence shows defendant added ev_241b442bed15_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-241b442bed15, Notamify mirror of OIIX A1010/26 and A1015/26. Stat and full read completed.
- Exact captured content in defendant evidence: A1010/26 period UTC 2026-05-22 20:46 to 2026-05-25 08:30 EST; A1015/26 replacement period UTC 2026-05-23 18:21 to 2026-05-25 08:30 EST. Text: all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except listed airports; exceptions operational sunrise to sunset; all previous permissions suspended for all operators; new permission requested/required from CAA/Iran CAA for civil IFR passenger flights. Q line QFALC and AD Airport Operations category.

Opposing argument to answer:
- Defense says raw mirror proves only airport operations, not broad airspace closure; exact NOTAM missing/inconsistent with secondary reports; airport alternative not proven.

Rebuttal analysis:
- Defendant evidence actually strengthens plaintiff on issuer/action/timing. It attributes permission system to CAA/Iran CAA, covers deadline, and gives exact broad airport closure wording.
- Rule tolerates limited exceptions and even identifies pre-approved Iranian CAA exceptions as permissible. The mirror's daylight exception airports and new CAA permission requirement are therefore not disqualifying.
- AD/airport category does not negate the airport-based alternative; rule includes complete suspension of arrivals/departures at at least two listed airports and broader closure of a major region. The mirror text says all airports in west Tehran FIR closed except named airports, and all prior permissions suspended for all operators.
- Key airport point: A1010/A1015 exceptions do not list OIIE (Imam Khomeini/IKA) or OIII (Mehrabad/THR); A1015 adds OIFM/Isfahan and others and includes OISS/Shiraz, but the Tehran listed airports appear outside the exception list. Be cautious about geography but argue this defeats defendant's claim that plaintiff cannot show listed-airport path.
- No new external evidence needed; rebuttal can use existing/defendant evidence. No new evidence submitted.

Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Surrebuttal work notes: Opportunity surrebuttals:defendant ready. Checked get_case, list_evidence, and stat_evidence for DX-1 ev_241b442bed15. No new evidence submitted by plaintiff in rebuttal; plaintiff reoffered PX-1/PX-2/PX-3 and technical report. Plaintiff rebuttal argues DX-1 supplies missing raw-wording context and largely confirms plaintiff: timing covers deadline, CAA/Iran CAA permission shows Iranian authority, all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except daylight exceptions, previous permissions suspended, and limited exceptions do not defeat broad closure.

Analysis: Need answer strongest point directly. Concede DX-1 confirms significant airport restrictions through deadline. Preserve defense distinction: market asks for a major closure of airspace or commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing from airspace/region, or complete suspension at two listed airports. DX-1 is AD Airport Operations/QFALC and its operative wording is airport-focused. It does not say western Tehran FIR/airspace closed or overflights suspended. “All airports in west part of Tehran FIR” uses FIR as geographic locator for airports, not necessarily an airspace closure. Permission language refers to civil IFR passenger flights on/at these airports and does not prove transit overflights suspended. Plaintiff’s limited-exception argument assumes a broad closure first; it does not prove one. Airport alternative remains unresolved because plaintiff’s reports conflict with DX-1 exception lists and there is no official airport-specific proof for two listed airports. No additional external source needed for surrebuttal; the issue is interpretation of admitted record. Cross-turn notes updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff closing private notes, arb-ex11a-20260630135427.

Opportunity: closings:plaintiff. Final action: deliver_closing_statement. No evidence or reports allowed.

Record refresh:
- get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence run. No new evidence after defendant Notamify mirror. Defendant surrebuttal filed, arguing airport-operations wording does not equal airspace/transit closure and listed-airport alternative remains unresolved.

Final record evidence:
- Market packet: rule allows broad closure/cancellation/suspension in Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region; limited CAA-approved exceptions do not disqualify; alternative for complete suspension affecting two listed airports.
- Plaintiff reports: Indian Express and RFE/RL identify Iranian aviation authority/CAA, A1010/26, May 22-May 25, western Tehran FIR/OIIX restrictions, severe passenger restrictions, daylight-only exceptions, and per-flight approval.
- Plaintiff context: SafeAirspace and EASA support OIIX/Tehran FIR context and non-weather conflict/security setting.
- Defense mirror: unofficial but raw wording; all airports in west part Tehran FIR closed except listed airports; all previous permissions suspended for all operators; civil IFR passenger flights require new CAA/Iran CAA permission; periods cover May 24 deadline.

Closing analysis:
- Strongest defense point: mirror is airport-operations NOTAM/QFALC and does not expressly say overflights/transit through FIR were closed. Also raw source is unofficial and exception lists create airport-specific uncertainty.
- Plaintiff response: standard is preponderance; the rule permits limited exceptions and focuses on major closure, not literal all-transit no-overflight. The combined record shows Iran-authority closure of all airports across the western Tehran FIR except controlled daylight exceptions, all previous permissions suspended, and new CAA permission for civil IFR passenger flights. That is a major non-weather closure under the market's practical definition. No weather evidence or foreign-only warning issue.
- Closing should be candid, concise, and ask for Yes.

Closing work notes: Opportunity closings:defendant ready. Waited through plaintiff closing. Used get_case/list_evidence in prompt and prior stat/read; no new evidence admitted in closing, and closing phase bars new evidence. Record now: market packet PX-1; plaintiff secondary reports PX-2/PX-3; plaintiff context PX-4/PX-5; defendant Notamify mirror DX-1; plaintiff and defendant arguments; plaintiff rebuttal; defendant surrebuttal; plaintiff closing. Plaintiff closing’s strongest point: preponderance not perfection; DX-1 is closest raw text and confirms all airports in west Tehran FIR closed except daylight/CAA-permission exceptions; commercial flights depend on airport operations; limited exceptions do not defeat broad closure.

Defense closing strategy: be candid that Iran likely imposed serious non-weather restrictions before deadline. Focus on exact market burden. The proposition requires a major closure of airspace within the rule: broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or major region, or complete suspension at two listed airports. The official Iranian AIS/CAA source remains missing. The closest raw wording is unofficial Notamify and is AD Airport Operations/QFALC; it says airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except specified airports, not FIR/airspace/overflights closed. Plaintiff cannot use the limited-exception clause to prove the threshold broad closure; it only prevents disqualification after broad closure is shown. Airport alternative remains unproven because reports and DX-1 conflict on which airports were operational/exceptions and some listed airports appear operational under controls. Closing should ask council to find restrictions proven but qualifying major closure not proven by preponderance. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
